reasoning: The market price for 'Elon Musk to be TIME's Person of the Year for 2025' was at 4% on October 16, 2025. Given that TIME rarely awards Person of the Year to the same individual multiple times within a few years (and Elon Musk already received the honor in 2021), the base rate for him winning again in 2025 is extremely low. Furthermore, a 4% market probability in late October suggests that there has been no dominant, overwhelmingly historic news event centered on Musk this year that would compel TIME to break their usual patterns. I'm choosing a slightly lower estimate of 3.5%, reflecting historical base rates for repeat winners among business figures.
